The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Authority to Sign Family Settlement Agreement for the Estate of Harriet Barbara Moskowitz.

The Court noted that the hearing had previously been continued at Ms. Balonoff's request and that she had filed a written objection.

Ms. Ballonoff requested that the matter be continued due to the unavailability of a court reporter and her own illness.

The Court noted that Ms. Ballonoff, as the sole beneficiary of the trust, had a direct financial interest in minimizing further costs associated with litigation and administration. Discussion was held regarding the potential for increased attorney and trustee fees if the matter were continued. The Court inquired whether Ms. Ballonoff was willing to withdraw her objection and permit the Court to review and decide the petition on its merits.

Ms. Ballonoff declined to withdraw her objection and stated her intention to proceed, and that she believed there were no funds remaining for distribution to her. Ms. Callejo confirmed that funds remained in the trust and that some distribution to Ms. Ballonoff would likely occur following payment of approved fees.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to December 29, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-12-295409 Conservatorship of Marilyn Silverman-Ballonoff and PTR-17-301171 The Marilyn Silverman-Ballonoff Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo appeared by zoom for and with Thomas Lucas (conservator/trustee) by zoom.
Charles Jonas (court appointed counsel) appeared by zoom for conservatee.
Selene Ballonoff in propria persona.
Teanna Ward (court reporter) appeared by zoom.

The court stated that it was trying to understand why the petition for assignment was filed in the trust rather than the conservatorship. Mrs. Callejo stated that the trust was created and that all of the funds for the conservatee are in her IOLTA account, that there is concern that if any money is distributed to the conservatee she would be ineligible for public benefits, and that this was done to preserve the conservatee's benefits. Discussion was held regarding the issue of whether or not Mrs. Moskowitz had been swindled, Mrs. Ballonoff having filed a petition for bill of review in Texas, Kemp & Associates not being the only show in town, Mrs. Ballonoff having contacted and negotiated with Drexel Heritage, the difference in the rates between the two companies, the issue of whether there is a conflict of interest with the fiduciary, there being no urgency, there being no documentation to establish a familial relationship, it not being clear whether there is anything to recover or not, Mrs. Ballonoff having failed to share any information regarding the mother's relationship, Mrs. Callejo having not been served with any objections for either matter, the pending discovery, Mrs. Ballonoff's concern regarding her mother's hip injury, Mrs. Ballonoff's position that Mr. Lucas did not address the issue of whether her mother is in a least restrictive means and that he never disclosed that her mother was moved to a skilled nursing facility, this matter not being on calendar for placement review or for termination, the court always retaining discretion to review fees, Mr. Jonas having no concerns about the petitions, etc. The Petition for Authority to Enter into an Assignment Agreement with Kemp & Associates, the Third Report and Account of Trustee, etc., and the Fifth Report of Conservator, etc. are under submission.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case no. PCN-12-295409, Conservatorship of Marilyn Silverman-Ballonoff.

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo, Esq. via Zoom for Thomas Lucas (Conservator/Trustee) via Zoom. Vincent Yu, Esq. via Zoom (Litigation Counsel) for Thomas Lucas. Selene Ballonoff in person, in propria persona.

The Court and Ms. Ballonoff briefly confer regarding her request for a court reporter for today's proceedings. Ms. Ballonoff stated that she doesn't always ask for a court reporter. The Court suggested that Ms. Ballonoff calendar the matter as a reminder, stated that it can't continue every hearing, and noted that Ms. Ballonoff is familiar with the court's expectations and rules. The Court also stated that in light of Ms. Ballonoff's request for a certified shorthad reporter that the matter is going to be continued. Ms. Ballonoff stated that she filed written objections today. Ms. Callejo noted that this has been Ms. Ballonoff's practice for the past nine years. Ms. Ballonoff stated that she is not good at legal procedure and that there should be a "vehicle" where she gives the Court facts. The Court stated that it only deals with the petitions before it and that all objections need to be filed before it can proceed. Ms. Ballonoff addressed her other pending proceedings and requested a continuance of six months. Ms. Callejo provided argument in opposition to a continuance of six months, stated that this matter has been tied up for years, that Ms. Ballonoff has taken money from her mother's conservatorship, that the Court should only continue the matter a short period of time, and that money is owed to the conservatorship. In response to the statement that money is owed to the conservatorship, the Court noted that the conservator could make that request. Ms. Ballonoff stated that she filed a bill of review in Texas and stated that she did not receive some of the examiner's notes. The Court addressed Ms. Ballonoff stating that complete objections must be filed by February 10, 2023. The Court continued all of the petitions before it today to February 21, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. All objections are to be filed and served by February 10, 2023, with courtesy copies to the Court by February 15, 2023. Brief discussions regarding Mr. Jonas' pending fee petiton on calendar for February 1, 2023, were held. The Court stated that Mr. Jonas' fee petition was not before it today and that if Ms. Ballonoff would be objecting that she should file and serve her objections to that petition one week prior to February 1, 2023.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

Appearances:
Selene Ballanoff in propria persona via Court Call.
Genevieve Callejo for and with Thomas Lucas.
Sheila Robella, Litigation Counsel, for Thomas Lucas.
Grant Tomioka, Court Appointed Counsel for Marilyn Silverman-Ballonoff.

11:30 AM - In-Camera Review hearing is held with the courtroom locked with only the counsel, parties, and court personnel present.

12:15 PM - Doors are unlocked at the conclusion of the in-camera review hearing to commence arguments on the petitions before the court.

Ms. Robello stated that responses have been filed, matters are ready to be submitted, matters are already briefed for trial, that Thomas Lucas is a professional, that decisions are made with the medical team weighing in, that the Conservatee is doing well, that the court appointed counsel repeatedly visited her, that Thomas Lucas made regular visits during the accounting period, Thomas Lucas had regular consultations with Kaiser doctors and nurse practitioners, that there was regular medical treatment at Buena Vista and that there is a Do Not Resuscitate on file for the Conservatee. Discussion was held as to whether the petition to limit authority of conservator was dismissed at the last hearing. The Court stated Ms. Ballonoff was to provide proof of service of the petition at this hearing. Ms. Ballonoff stated she was not aware of the proof of service and is not ready with it. Ms. Robello stated she is ready to go forward and estimates two half days. The Court stated it will need a joint status report from all parties as to the issues to be tried, dispute of facts, witness list with time estimate as well as an overall time estimate.

Ms. Ballonoff stated they are hiding UCSF records, that Mr. Lucas fired the Harvard trained doctor without pay and replaced her with visiting nurses on palliative care without treatment, that Thomas Lucas want the funds to pay himself and attorney, that his services are not for the sole benefit of her mother, that there are no provisions if there is a settlement or leftover funds, that her mother's activity consisted of walking the halls, that walking the halls is not a activity but a cry for activity, that Thomas Lucas has been silent on her emails to him about her mother's lack of a coat, shoes and bra, that she offered to return money if it meant her mother would be in a nursing facility, that their caseload is out of proportion with their resources, and that she could care for her mother in her home, and she could do better with treatment without the stress of these proceedings.

The First Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is continued to April 13, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Parties are to meet and confer regarding trial plan and a joint status report is to be filed by April 03, 2020.

The Application for Authorization to Set Aside Funds into Attorneys Trust Account for Expenses of Administration and Litigation in order to Qualify for and Preserve Settlors Public Benefits is taken under submission.

The following matters were heard concurrently:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo for Thomas Lucas (Conservator).
Natalie Lupo for Thomas Lucas.
Salene Ballonoff in Propria Persona.

Ms. Callejo stated that she has a declaration prepared for Mr. Lucas to sign, that they are going to back out the retirement accounts ($30,000.00) and will account for them in the conservatorship, and that the bank records for those accounts were filed in the trust matter. Ms. Callejo also stated that she will respond to the Probate Examiner's notes, that Mr. Lucas was unable to respond and that she may file an amendment to the accounting. The Court stated that the objections seem to be stand alone and that it does not see any carry over to all of the other petitions. Ms. Ballonoff raised the issue of the filing fee for the petition to reinstate the advanced health care directive appearing in the trust matter and the conservatorship, stated that Ms. Callejo took it off calendar, that it is of no value to her Mother, that Ms. Callejo should not be paid for her time related to the petition and that there are a lot of bills for medication and the assisted living facility. Ms. Callejo stated that Mr. Lucas does not provide medications, that he follows the guidance of the medical professionals, that she does not feel this is an accounting issue and that she believes the Court can decide this without an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Callejo also stated that she filed the petition, that when conferring with Court appointed Counsel they decided to take the matter off calendar, that this does happen, that the Probate Examiner has no issue with her request for fees for said petition and that she does not feel they should revisit the issue of the trust being established. Ms. Lupo raised the issue of motion to compel filed by Ms. Ballonoff, stated that there three sets of discovery have been served, that the third set was served on Ms. Ballonoff last week, that Ms. Ballonoff has threatened to file a motion to compel in the conservatorship as well and that she is considering a protective order for her Client to prevent continued harassment. The Court stated that all of the issues with breach of fiduciary duty and all of the pending petitions should be heard at the same time and need to go out to Dept. 206 for trial (trial is to be set when discovery is completed). The Petition to Limit Authority of Conservator, etc., Petition to Modify Authority of Conservator and the First Report and Account of Trustee, etc. are continued to July 10, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a status conference and for status on filing of the amended accounting.
